title: Align Sparkle update preference and mascot app icon
description: Make the Settings update preference control Sparkle's scheduler directly, removing duplicate manual scheduling. Package the existing Flare mascot in every macOS AppIcon slot.
acceptance_criteria: Automatic checks respect the setting without a second timer, and the AppIcon catalog has valid 16 through 1024px mascot assets.
close_reason: Completed: bound the preference to Sparkle's automatic scheduler, removed the duplicate timer, and regenerated all macOS AppIcon assets from the mascot source.

title: Prioritize verified embedded ATS endpoints for later refreshes
description: A wrapper careers page can expose a direct Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, or Ashby board URL in escaped page data. Detection must normalize and verify that board URL, persist it on the board config, and use the dedicated fetcher before generic extraction or AI on later refreshes.
acceptance_criteria: Escaped provider URLs are detected; Workday job links normalize to their board root; added boards persist the verified provider URL and later refreshes select the provider fetcher first.
close_reason: Hardened embedded ATS URL scanning for escaped JSON URLs. Existing board configuration persistence and fetch ordering already send later refreshes directly to the verified provider URL.

title: Parse embedded application records before AI fallback
description: Career pages such as ABB serialize complete job records in the document with applyUrl, title, location, and job ID. The deterministic detector only inspected JSON-LD and job anchors, so these boards could be labeled AI Parsing despite having usable structured data.
acceptance_criteria: Embedded applyUrl records produce a deterministic preview, are labeled Embedded JSON, and do not enter the AI fallback.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic extraction of embedded applyUrl job records, including ABB's Phenom payload, before the AI fallback.

title: Make deterministic board detection evidence-scored before AI fallback
description: Replace the fragile detector waterfall with observable evidence scoring, validation, and bounded AI fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Detector records evidence and confidence, validates proposed extraction routes, caches successful routes with expiry, and exposes actionable outcomes.
close_reason: Completed evidence-scored deterministic ATS detection, route validation, bounded AI fallback, expiring successful-route cache, and visible detection evidence.

title: Strengthen listing-to-detail job description enrichment
description: Improve job description completeness by separating low-cost listing discovery from lazy per-job detail enrichment and caching validated results.
acceptance_criteria: Jobs with abbreviated descriptions can be enriched from trustworthy detail sources; extraction is cached with provenance/freshness and failure is non-destructive.
close_reason: Completed lazy per-job description enrichment with JSON-LD/main-content extraction, 14-day provenance cache, single-flight requests, and non-destructive fallback.

title: Replace generic utility-panel UI with an authored Flare visual system
description: Rework the visual language across the primary job, board, and settings experiences to feel warm, utilitarian, and deliberately authored rather than a generic developer dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Shared visual tokens and components establish a cohesive distinctive look; core screens use the system; interaction states remain accessible and practical.
close_reason: Completed authored paper-and-ember visual system across core job, board, and settings screens, including mascot asset and accessible states.

title: Repair UI test host target after application rename
description: UI-test configurations still target MSJobMonitor although the application target is FlareJobMonitor.
acceptance_criteria: Both Debug and Release UI-test configurations identify FlareJobMonitor, allowing UI tests to launch the app target.
notes: Updated Debug and Release UI-test host settings to FlareJobMonitor.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Honor configured page limits for TikTok and prevent polling intervals below fetch duration
description:
## Problem
JobManager.fetchFromSource passes maxPages: 350 for TikTok regardless of the user's maxPagesToFetch setting (JobsManager.swift:542-557). TikTokFetcher sleeps 300 ms between pages (TikTokJobFetcher.swift:116), so a worst-case run spends roughly 105 seconds sleeping/requesting. Monitoring permits a 60-second interval (JobsManager.swift:497-501), guaranteeing overlapping TikTok work and sustained network/CPU use when responses paginate deeply.

## Recommended fix
Pass the configured bounded page limit to TikTok, set source-specific sane hard caps, and have the refresh coordinator schedule the next run only after the prior source run completes (with backoff on failures/rate limits). Surface an estimate/warning for unusually high page limits and test that a 1-minute setting cannot overlap a long TikTok fetch.
notes: TikTok now honors maxPagesToFetch with a hard cap of 25 pages instead of always requesting 350.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Bound WebKit interception payloads and guarantee renderer cleanup on navigation failure
description:
## Problem
WebKitRenderer injects JavaScript that retains up to 1,000,000 characters for every fetch/XHR response in window.__apiCalls (WebKitRenderer.swift:75-77, 111-113), then serializes the entire array plus full document HTML into Swift (209-217). This can retain many MB per board and drives expensive JS serialization. On navigation failure, the delegate resumes continuation (which is unused by renderWithAPIDetection) instead of renderContinuation and does not nil webView (189-205), so render callers can hang/retain a WKWebView until incidental timeout behavior.

## Recommended fix
Capture only candidate job API responses, impose strict per-response and total byte/count budgets, discard bodies immediately after a matching route is selected, and avoid serializing full interception history. Unify completion in a finish method that resumes exactly the active continuation once, stops loading, cancels the timeout, clears delegates/webView, and handles Task cancellation. Instrument renderer memory and elapsed time.
notes: Implementation started: bounding WebKit interception data and unifying continuation cleanup in WebKitRenderer.swift only.
close_reason: Implemented in Flare/Services/LLM/WebKitRenderer.swift. The interception script now retains at most 12 candidate job/API calls, caps request bodies at 16 KiB, responses at 128 KiB, headers at 30 entries/value 1 KiB, and all captured metadata/payloads at 512 KiB. Renderer work now has cancellation handling and one finish path that stops loading, clears delegates/scripts, cancels timeout, nils the web view, and resumes the render continuation exactly once for success, navigation failures, extraction errors, and cancellation. Validation: xcrun swiftc -parse Flare/Services/LLM/WebKitRenderer.swift; git diff --check. Full xcodebuild unavailable because active developer directory is CommandLineTools.

title: Serialize refresh work across manual, per-source, custom-board, and wake triggers
description:
## Problem
JobManager launches independent unstructured Tasks from seven per-source Timer callbacks (JobsManager.swift:494-501), the didWake observer (510-518), and Refresh Now. fetchAllJobs (347-414) has no in-flight guard and also calls JobBoardMonitor.fetchAllBoardJobs; JobBoardMonitor owns a second repeating timer (103-110) that calls the same board fetch independently. The resulting overlapping runs mutate jobsBySource, storedJobIds, allJobs, fetchStatistics, and persistence in completion order.

## Evidence
- A minimum 1-minute interval is allowed, but individual fetches can take longer.
- Wake begins a complete fetch without cancelling/debouncing timer-launched work.
- stopMonitoring invalidates timers only; already-created Tasks and URLSession work continue and can persist results after stop/clear.

## Recommended fix
Introduce one cancellation-aware refresh coordinator/actor with a single in-flight task (or per-source coalescing keyed by source) and monotonically increasing generation token. Route manual refresh, timers, board refresh, wake, and cache clear through it. Cancel/coalesce stale requests, prevent commits from stale generations, expose pending/running state to disable conflicting UI, and test concurrent triggers.
notes: Implemented a single in-flight refresh gate, stale-generation checks, cache-clear exclusion, and a JobManager-owned custom-board timer.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Make persisted job state crash-safe and observable
description: Persistence currently uses non-atomic writes and suppresses critical save failures, which can corrupt cache state or hide data loss.
acceptance_criteria: Job and tracking writes are atomic; failures are propagated or surfaced appropriately; cache cleanup leaves no partial logical state; automated tests cover normal and error paths.
notes: Writes now use Data.WritingOptions.atomic; cache cleanup stages and rolls back moves; added persistence cleanup coverage.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Store downloaded AI model under the app support directory
description: ModelDownloader.getModelPath walks up from Application Support and appends Data/Library/Application Support/Flare. In non-sandbox builds this points under ~/Data/... instead of ~/Library/Application Support/Flare; in sandbox-style paths it can duplicate Data. The Settings model status and LLM load path depend on this computed path.
acceptance_criteria: Downloaded model path is derived from FileManager.applicationSupportDirectory/Flare consistently with PersistenceService, works for sandboxed and non-sandboxed builds, and any old misplaced model is migrated or detected with a clear recovery path.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Persist per-source refresh results even when no new jobs are found
description: JobManager.fetchJobsFromSource updates jobsBySource, then only calls processNewJobs when newJobs is non-empty. Timer refreshes that find zero new IDs do not recompute allJobs, update fetch statistics, save changed jobs, or remove disappeared postings.
acceptance_criteria: A per-source timer refresh updates allJobs, fetchStatistics, lastFetchTime, and persisted jobs for that source even when newJobs is empty. Notifications are still sent only for genuinely new or bumped jobs.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Tie custom board monitor lifecycle to global monitoring state
description: JobManager.startMonitoring starts JobBoardMonitor when custom boards are enabled, but JobManager.stopMonitoring only invalidates built-in source timers. JobBoardMonitor keeps its own 30-minute timer running, and enableCustomBoards changes are not part of sourceBindings.
acceptance_criteria: Stopping monitoring invalidates custom board monitoring too. Saving settings with custom boards disabled stops the custom-board timer; enabling it starts the timer only when monitoring is active. Custom board fetch interval uses the same configured refresh interval or a clearly separate setting.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Honor Include Remote Jobs setting across fetchers
description: Settings exposes includeRemoteJobs and says it automatically adds remote to location searches, but the fetchers currently add remote unconditionally or hard-code includeRemote=1. Turning the toggle off does not actually exclude remote searches.
acceptance_criteria: Remote augmentation is controlled by JobManager.includeRemoteJobs. When disabled, fetchers do not add remote/anywhere keywords or includeRemote=1 unless the user explicitly typed a remote keyword. Behavior is covered for Microsoft, Google, Greenhouse, Lever, iCIMS, Taleo, and custom-board fetch paths.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Do not invoke LLM when direct universal extraction already found jobs
description: UniversalJobFetcher calls tryLLMExtraction whenever AI is enabled and JSON/API extraction found jobs, just to compare counts. This spends a full model pass on successful simple/direct routes.
acceptance_criteria: If universal JSON/API extraction returns matching jobs, the fetcher returns them without invoking LLM. LLM is only used when direct extraction finds no jobs or explicit fallback discovery is needed.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Let LLMParser idle-unload manage model lifetime
description: SmartJobParser manually calls unloadModel after successful and failed branches even though LLMParser acquire/release already schedules idle unload. This can reload the 3B model repeatedly across boards in one refresh.
acceptance_criteria: SmartJobParser no longer force-unloads after parser calls; model remains warm across nearby LLM operations and unloads through LLMParser's idle timer.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Cache simple API extraction routes separately from HTML extraction
description: Simple API extraction success is cached by setting htmlExtractionWorks, so later refreshes render WebKit again to rediscover the API route instead of fetching the known endpoint directly. This makes existing autodetected API routes look like full refetches in logs.
acceptance_criteria: When a simple API endpoint is known, refresh attempts direct cached endpoint extraction before WebKit rendering; WebKit is only used if the cached endpoint fails.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Use stable IDs for AI, HTML, and schema-extracted jobs
description: UUID-based IDs in SmartJobParser, JobBoardMonitor quick extraction, and UniversalJobFetcher cause the same extracted posting to be treated as new on every refresh. Use deterministic IDs from provider ID when present, otherwise canonical job URL/title/company/location hash.
acceptance_criteria: Repeated fetches of the same AI/HTML/schema extracted board produce the same job IDs; no duplicate persisted jobs or repeated new-job notifications for unchanged postings.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Wire iCIMS and Taleo through AI-detected ATS fetch paths
description: Direct board detection can preview iCIMS/Taleo, but SmartJobParser.fetchFromDetectedATS and JobBoardMonitor cached detectedATS refresh dispatch only Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, and Ashby.
acceptance_criteria: iCIMS and Taleo detected via script/WebKit/LLM paths can fetch immediately and continue fetching after detectedATSURL/detectedATSType are persisted.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Remove visible corner background from sidebar mascot
description: The sidebar mascot used a rounded-square raster canvas, making dark corner pixels visible against the sidebar. Preserve the existing artwork and expose it through a circular alpha mask.
acceptance_criteria: Mascot asset has transparent exterior corners while the circular badge and artwork remain unchanged.
close_reason: Completed: created a circular alpha-masked mascot asset, updated the asset catalog, and verified transparent corners in a successful Xcode build.

title: Preserve MainActor isolation for custom board parser status callbacks
description: JobBoardMonitor passes MainActor closures to a Sendable callback, producing Swift 6 actor-isolation warnings.
acceptance_criteria: Status updates hop explicitly to MainActor and Xcode tests build without this warning.
notes: Status callbacks now explicitly hop to MainActor through a child task.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Remove Swift 6 actor-isolation warning from API schema cache initialization
description: APISchemaCache calls an actor-isolated instance load method from its initializer, producing a Swift 6 concurrency warning during builds.
acceptance_criteria: APISchemaCache initializes its cached state without actor-isolation warnings and Xcode tests pass.
notes: Refactored initialization to a static cache loader and made schema writes atomic.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Quiesce monitoring for system sleep and debounce refresh after wake
description:
## Problem
Flare observes only NSWorkspace.didWakeNotification (JobsManager.swift:510-518). It neither cancels/suspends outstanding work on sleep nor resets timer schedules, then starts a full fetch immediately on wake. Timers scheduled on the main run loop and in-flight URLSession/WebKit work can resume at the same time, creating burst CPU/network work and racing the normal schedules.

## Recommended fix
Observe willSleep and didWake. On sleep, pause monitoring and cancel/cooperatively stop refresh and renderer work; on wake, reschedule timers from now and enqueue exactly one debounced refresh after connectivity settles. Do not resume if monitoring was disabled before sleep. Add lifecycle tests for sleep during a fetch, repeated wake notifications, and disabled monitoring.
notes:
Added will-sleep quiescing, timer suspension, stale-generation protection, timer rescheduling, and one five-second debounced wake refresh.
Changed the post-wake path to wait for sessionDidBecomeActive plus 15 seconds; a 60-second fallback prevents a wake refresh while the login screen remains active.
close_reason: Completed: wake refresh now waits for active user session plus a settling delay, with a 60-second fallback when no session activation notification arrives.

title: Improve custom board addition, detection, and testing flow
description: Make adding a job board clearer and safer across direct ATS detection, preview/testing, and failure states.
acceptance_criteria: Users can understand each step, see detection/test state and errors, and complete add/edit flows without losing existing behavior.
notes: Completed board-management and add/detect/test flow redesign with stale-result and duplicate protection.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Redesign Settings information architecture and storage actions
description: Replace the dense settings layout with a clearer, accessible control hierarchy. Preserve settings behavior and make model/storage/cache states easy to understand.
acceptance_criteria: All existing settings remain available; cache cleanup has clear confirmation, progress/result feedback, and accessible labels; the layout is visually coherent at common macOS window sizes.
notes: Completed Settings hierarchy, storage/cache controls, progress feedback, and accessibility pass.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with Xcode tests.

title: Align app metadata and sandbox network capabilities
description: The app still had stale Microsoft tooltip/About version text, Sparkle automatic checks stored as a string, an ATS exception value with the wrong type, and project network capability flags that did not match the entitlements used for a network-fetching app.
acceptance_criteria: Branding uses Flare, About reads the bundle version, Info.plist values have expected plist types, outgoing network is enabled for the app target, and unused incoming network entitlement is removed.
close_reason: Implemented in mini audit pass; verified with swiftc -parse and plutil -lint

title: Do not mark Amazon supported without a fetcher
description: JobSource.amazon was marked supported, but JobManager and JobBoardMonitor have no Amazon fetcher implementation. Detected Amazon URLs could appear supported and then produce no jobs.
acceptance_criteria: Amazon is shown as unsupported/coming soon until an actual fetcher path exists.
close_reason: Implemented in mini audit pass; verified with swiftc -parse and plutil -lint

title: Honor remote toggle in TikTok fetcher
description: TikTok still performed an automatic remote keyword fetch regardless of includeRemoteJobs after other fetchers had been wired to the setting.
acceptance_criteria: TikTok only runs the extra remote keyword pass when Include Remote Jobs is enabled or the user explicitly searches for remote.
close_reason: Implemented in mini audit pass; verified with swiftc -parse and plutil -lint

title: Avoid duplicate fetch from Save and Refresh Now
description: Settings.saveSettings called startMonitoring, which already fetches, and the Save and Refresh Now button then called fetchAllJobs again. This could produce two immediate full refreshes.
acceptance_criteria: Save Settings only persists and resyncs active timers. Save and Refresh Now persists settings and performs one immediate refresh.
close_reason: Implemented in mini audit pass; verified with swiftc -parse and plutil -lint

title: Open Settings tab on first launch
description: First launch set JobManager.showSettings, but no view observed that property, so the app still opened on the Jobs tab.
acceptance_criteria: First launch selects the Settings tab directly and no dead showSettings state remains.
close_reason: Implemented in mini audit pass; verified with swiftc -parse and plutil -lint

title: Refresh job list when applied or starred state changes
description: JobListView recalculates filtered rows on search/source/status-toggle/allJobs changes, but not when JobManager.appliedJobIds or starredJobIds change. With the Starred or Applied filter enabled, toggling a row/detail action can leave the list showing stale rows until another filter change or refresh occurs.
acceptance_criteria: JobListView observes appliedJobIds and starredJobIds changes and recomputes the filtered list while preserving pagination sensibly. Row badges and filtered membership update immediately after star/apply actions.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Register intended first-run defaults for built-in sources
description: Built-in source toggles use UserDefaults.bool(forKey:), which defaults to false when unset. SettingsView state initializes several sources to true, but loadSettings immediately overwrites those values from JobManager, so fresh installs can start with all built-in sources disabled.
acceptance_criteria: App startup registers explicit defaults for built-in sources and settings. First launch shows and uses the intended enabled source set consistently across JobManager and SettingsView.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Validate custom board URLs before detection and import
description: The Add Board form treats URL(string:) != nil as valid, and JobBoardConfig.init does not reject malformed or unsupported URLs. Strings without an http(s) scheme/host can pass validation and later fail detection or cache under unusable values.
acceptance_criteria: Custom board add/import paths only accept http or https URLs with a host, normalize whitespace and scheme casing, and surface a user-facing validation error before detection. Existing valid configs continue to load.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Load persisted job state independently and tolerate missing files
description: JobManager.loadStoredData wraps jobs, stored IDs, applied IDs, and starred IDs in one do/catch. PersistenceService.loadJobs, loadStoredJobIds, and loadAppliedJobIds throw when files are missing, so a fresh install or one missing/corrupt file can prevent unrelated state from loading.
acceptance_criteria: Missing jobs/stored/applied/starred files are treated as empty. Each persisted state file loads independently so failure in one file does not prevent the others from loading. Corrupt-file errors remain visible in logs or UI diagnostics.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Reschedule active fetch timers when refresh interval changes
description: refreshInterval is persisted on change, but already scheduled Timer instances keep their original interval. Settings.saveSettings calls startMonitoring, which reschedules built-in source timers, but direct property changes and JobBoardMonitor's separate hard-coded timer still do not consistently follow the new interval.
acceptance_criteria: Changing refreshInterval reschedules all active built-in source timers and the custom-board timer without requiring app restart. Invalid or tiny values are clamped to a safe minimum. Existing monitoring state is preserved.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Avoid returning unfiltered AI/WebKit extraction results when filters remove all jobs
description: SmartJobParser returns unfiltered jobs when title/location filters remove every extracted result. That can make custom board refresh ignore user filters and import noisy boards.
acceptance_criteria: Fallback extraction respects filters consistently; if filters remove all jobs, the parser reports no matching jobs instead of returning the unfiltered set.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Improve non-LLM JSON extraction before schema discovery
description: trySimpleAPIExtraction only checks top-level arrays and a few top-level keys. Many career APIs nest jobs under data.search.results, props.pageProps, nodes, edges, or similar containers, forcing unnecessary LLM schema discovery.
acceptance_criteria: Parser can find likely job dictionaries recursively within bounded JSON depth and extract jobs without LLM when title/url/id/location fields are present.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse

title: Replay intercepted WebKit API requests more faithfully
description: Simple API extraction currently requires absolute URLs and loses POST request bodies. Relative intercepted endpoints and POST/GraphQL searches can be dropped or replayed as empty requests.
acceptance_criteria: Simple extraction resolves relative API URLs against the page URL, preserves request method/body/content-type where available, and skips non-JSON/non-job endpoints cheaply.
close_reason: Implemented fixes and verified with swiftc -parse
